# Bridge orchestrator

## The scenario

Toronto → Windsor along the 401, generated at a 60-second cadence with three injected gaps:

- ~6 minutes / ~10 km
- ~11 minutes / ~18 km
- ~16 minutes / ~27 km

We match the same trip three ways: clean, gappy without bridging, gappy with bridging.

## 1. Clean trip — baseline

![](figures/bridge-clean.png){.nostretch fig-align="center" width="80%"}

No gaps, no surprises. One continuous matched shape from Toronto to Windsor.

## 2. Gaps + `breakage_distance=3000`

![](figures/bridge-no-bridge.png){.nostretch fig-align="center" width="80%"}

Breakage threshold below every injected gap → Meili splits at each one → **four disjoint matched shapes**. Cranking `breakage_distance` up rejoins them but lets Meili invent a path across the gap; safe on a highway, dangerous on a city grid.

## 3. Gaps + bridge orchestrator

![](figures/bridge-with-bridge.png){.nostretch fig-align="center" width="80%"}

`map_match_dataframe_with_bridges`: split at every qualifying gap → match each sub-segment separately → bridge the gap with one `/route` + `edge_walk`. Blue = matched, dotted red = bridges. Continuous polyline, every metre either a real ping or an explicit routing decision.

## Knobs — `BridgeConfig`

| Parameter | Default | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `max_dist_m` | 5 000 m | distance trigger — any gap farther splits |
| `time_s` | 240 s | time trigger — only if also moved … |
| `min_dist_m` | 1 000 m | … at least this far during the gap |

```
split if (dist > max_dist_m)  OR  (dt > time_s  AND  dist > min_dist_m)
```

`min_dist_m` floor on the time rule keeps red-light stalls from triggering.

## Detour ratio

Each successful bridge reports **detour ratio** = routed distance ÷ straight-line distance.

- Close to **1.0** → bridge is essentially the straight line (highway gap, safe).
- Well above **1.0** → Valhalla had to detour, treat the bridged path with suspicion.

## When to use it

- **Highway trips with intermittent dropouts** — bridges are cheap and accurate
- **Urban trips with large gaps** — prefer to leave the trip split; routing across a 5 km city gap invents most of its own path
- **Devices that buffer and dump** — bridge first, then the downstream pipeline only ever sees one trip per actual trip
